skill.name: nft-standards
skill.description: Guides implementation of ERC-721 and ERC-1155 NFT contracts, metadata formats, royalties, soulbound tokens, dynamic NFTs, and marketplace integration patterns.
skill.summary: Build ERC-721 and ERC-1155 NFT contracts with metadata, royalties, minting, and marketplace patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk because Solidity require statements, Markdown code fences, NFT standard names, and Base64 metadata examples matched generic malware patterns. Manual review found no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent; remaining concerns are copy-paste contract hardening issues.

low_findings.title: False positive: Solidity require statements flagged as dynamic require
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are Solidity require checks in NFT contract examples. They validate mint quantity, supply caps, payment, authorization, and royalty limits; they do not load modules or execute dynamic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is inside a Solidity code block and uses the require(condition, message) assertion form. No JavaScript require call or variable-controlled import is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown code fences flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick lines open or close Markdown examples for Solidity and JSON. They are documentation delimiters, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain Markdown code fence syntax around examples. The skill file is documentation and does not define runnable shell code at those locations.

low_findings.title: False positive: NFT terms and Base64 metadata flagged as blocker patterns
low_findings.description: The flagged high-risk keywords occur in NFT descriptions, royalty interface names, comments, and metadata examples. Base64 appears only as an NFT metadata encoding pattern, not as obfuscated payload execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is ERC standards and metadata construction. No decoded payload, outbound request, command execution, or persistence behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Illustrative contract snippets need production hardening
low_findings.description: Some examples are simplified for teaching and should not be copied directly into production. Examples include a transfer-based withdrawal pattern and a soulbound mint function without access control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show concrete Solidity patterns that can be inappropriate in production. They are not malicious, but users should review access control, withdrawal design, and reentrancy protections before deployment.

content.value_statement: NFT contracts often fail on metadata, royalties, and minting rules. This skill provides focused ERC-721 and ERC-1155 patterns for safer implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use ERC-721 versus ERC-1155 for NFT projects.
Provides Solidity examples for minting, burning, metadata, royalties, soulbound tokens, and dynamic NFTs.
Shows IPFS metadata structure with attributes and media references.
Covers EIP-2981 royalty integration for marketplace compatibility.
Introduces gas-optimized ERC721A minting for large collections.
Lists marketplace integration considerations for major NFT platforms.
limitations: The examples are instructional and require security review before deployment.
It does not include complete tests, deployment scripts, or audit reports.
Some referenced resources are described but not included in this single-file skill package.
It does not verify current marketplace policy changes or legal royalty enforceability.

use_cases.title: Launch a Collectible NFT Contract
use_cases.description: Use ERC-721 patterns for fixed supply, priced minting, token URI generation, and owner withdrawals.

use_cases.title: Design Game Item Tokens
use_cases.description: Use ERC-1155 patterns for multiple item types, batch minting, supply tracking, and player burns.

use_cases.title: Prepare Marketplace-Compatible Metadata
use_cases.description: Structure metadata, attributes, IPFS links, royalties, and marketplace behavior before launch.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Token Standard
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new NFT project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between ERC-721 and ERC-1155 for a project with [collection type], [supply model], and [transfer needs].

prompt_templates.title: Draft Minting Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minting plan for an NFT contract with max supply, price, per-wallet limits, reveal timing, and withdrawal requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Review NFT Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my NFT metadata plan for IPFS storage, attributes, media fields, rarity traits, and marketplace compatibility risks.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Contract Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my ERC-721 or ERC-1155 design for access control, supply invariants, royalty behavior, reentrancy risk, and upgrade concerns.

output_examples.input: I need a collectible NFT with paid public minting and marketplace royalties.
output_examples.output: A recommended ERC-721 design with fixed supply, price checks, per-mint limits, IPFS metadata, EIP-2981 royalty support, and production hardening notes.

output_examples.input: I am building a game with swords, shields, and consumable items.
output_examples.output: A recommended ERC-1155 design with separate token IDs, per-item supply caps, batch minting, burn rules, and metadata URI planning.

output_examples.input: I want tokens that cannot be transferred after issuance.
output_examples.output: A soulbound token approach that blocks transfers while allowing minting and controlled burning, with access control review points.

best_practices: Start from audited OpenZeppelin contracts and document every override.
Pin metadata and media before launch, then test marketplace rendering.
Review minting, withdrawal, royalty, and access-control logic before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy the sample snippets without tests and security review.
Do not rely on mutable off-chain metadata without a clear reveal or freeze policy.
Do not leave minting, burning, or royalty updates unrestricted.

faq.question: Does this skill generate production-ready NFT contracts?
faq.answer: It provides implementation patterns and examples. Production contracts still need tests, audits, deployment review, and project-specific controls.

faq.question: Does it support both ERC-721 and ERC-1155?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains both standards and shows examples for single-token collections and multi-token item systems.

faq.question: Can it help with NFT metadata?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers IPFS metadata, attributes, on-chain metadata concepts, and dynamic metadata patterns.

faq.question: Does it include royalty guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes EIP-2981 royalty concepts and marketplace compatibility considerations.

faq.question: Can it build marketplace backends?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on standards, contract patterns, metadata, and integration considerations, not full marketplace infrastructure.

faq.question: Is external code executed by this skill?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains a Markdown skill file with Solidity and JSON examples, not executable helper scripts.
